strategy: Buy $145 Call — Aug 2026 (enter after May 4 earnings)

wheelStrategy.ineligibleReason: Stock price $144.08 — 1 CSP contract requires ~$14,400 cash. Exceeds $10,000 per-contract threshold for a standard retail portfolio. Use Calls/Spreads strategy only.
wheelStrategy.vixNote: VIX at 16.89 — BELOW 20 threshold. Wait for VIX > 20 before entering premium-selling positions.
wheelStrategy.bollingerEntry: N/A — Wheel not applicable for this stock

exitRules.entryTrigger: Enter now (post May 5 earnings, IV crush has settled to ~55–60%). Stock at $134.70 — bought the post-earnings dip. Target $145 call.
exitRules.profitExit: Close at 60–90% gain on premium, OR when stock hits $165 (Contract 1 target). Let 1 contract ride toward $190 for 180–220% gain.
exitRules.timeStop: If stock has not cleared $150+ by ~June 20 (50% of time elapsed on Sep position), close. Do not fight accelerating theta in the final 30 days.
exitRules.lossStop: Close at 40–50% loss on premium paid. Do not hold a decaying OTM position.
exitRules.earningsHardStop: Close by July 24 (5 days before July 29 Q2 earnings) — no exceptions for long premium positions.

options.bestFor: Only if expecting a swift move within 4–5 weeks. 22 days left at close makes theta brutal — almost no time value when you must exit.
Sweet spot — 57 days of time value at the July 24 exit window. Enough runway for the bull thesis to play out. IV at 57% makes single-leg feasible.
Two earnings cycles of coverage. Expensive for a name with a clear near-term catalyst window — overpaying for time you likely won't use.

recommendation.title: Single-Leg Call — ATM, Enter Post-Earnings
recommendation.entryNote: ⚠️ Q1 earnings May 4 after close — IV at 80–87%, options pricing ~10.55% expected move. ENTER AFTER earnings to avoid IV crush. Post-earnings IV likely drops to 55–65%. Bull thesis: AIP commercial acceleration, $300M USDA deal, government reacceleration. Consensus expects +115% EPS YoY and $1.54B revenue (+74% YoY).

companyOverview: Leading AI data analytics platform for government and enterprise. AIP (Artificial Intelligence Platform) driving explosive commercial adoption. Q1 2026 earnings on May 4: consensus expects revenue $1.54B (+74% YoY), EPS $0.28 (+115% YoY). $300M USDA deal (April 22) expanding into civilian government. Stock near 52-week low ($105.32) despite strong fundamentals — 33% upside to analyst consensus.
catalysts: $300M USDA 5-year deal (April 22) — 'One Farmer, One File' civilian government expansion
AIP commercial segment: expected +94% YoY — the key growth driver
Citi upgraded to Buy with $235 target; Oppenheimer initiated Buy at $200
Stock near 52-week low vs. strong fundamental momentum — asymmetric setup
Full-year guidance raise expected after Q1 beat
risks: IV crush risk if entering before May 4 earnings — IV 80–87% drops to 55–65% post-event
Valuation concerns at $344B market cap — requires sustained 74%+ revenue growth
Dependence on AIP commercial acceleration continuing at record pace
Government contract concentration risk — changes in administration policy
Stock down ~31% from $207.52 52-week high — sentiment fragile
dilution: Palantir has significant SBC (stock-based compensation) as % of revenue. Management and employee dilution is ongoing but well-understood and priced in by market.
